title: Federated learning and Indigenous genomic data sovereignty
methods: privacy-preserving ML review (differential privacy, SMPC, homomorphic encryption)

data_scope.coverage: global perspective; implementation context primarily North American with explicit call for global extension

core_conclusion: Open-data policies are fundamentally incompatible with Indigenous data sovereignty and risk perpetuating colonial data extraction in genomics research. Federated learning — which trains ML models on distributed community-held nodes without exposing raw data — is structurally compatible with OCAP and CARE governance frameworks and can empower communities to participate in precision-medicine research while retaining ownership and control of their genomic data.
why_it_matters: Names the structural contradiction between NIH open-data mandates and Indigenous data sovereignty; quantifies the genomic divide (<1% vs ~7% global population); connects FL engineering to UNDRIP rights; honestly flags technical limits of differential privacy for genomics; extends the argument globally beyond North America.
why_nature: Policy intervention at the ML-society interface in a high-impact venue; timely commentary on active NIH initiatives; bridges FL engineering with bioethics and Indigenous rights law; Brief Communication genre suited for field-level norm setting.
added_value_to_my_research: Privacy-audit protocol (gradient-inversion / membership-inference tests) applicable to multi-source spatiotemporal data fusion with sensitive mobility traces
Revocable spatially-scoped consent + machine unlearning → community-controlled health data observatories at tract / tribal-boundary granularity
2SFCA / R2SFCA applied to precision-medicine facility access across Indigenous lands → spatial decomposition of genomic underrepresentation into geographic vs. policy drivers
Indigenous relational epistemologies as graph-structural priors in GNN → community-epistemology-informed spatial prediction beyond administrative boundaries
Consortium node-placement as facility-location optimization → R2SFCA / Monte Carlo framework applied to equitable distributed health-data infrastructure design

related.title: Collaborative learning without sharing data
related.relation: directly related federated learning paper for privacy-preserving medical ML without data pooling
